records.ety: [OE. thirst, þurst, AS. þurst, þyrst; akin to D. dorst, OS. thurst, G. durst, Icel. þorsti, Sw. & Dan. törst, Goth. þarstei thirst, þarsus dry, withered, þarsieþ mik I thirst, gaþarsan to wither, L. torrere to parch, Gr. te`rsesqai to become dry, tesai`nein to dry up, Skr. tsh to thirst. √54. Cf. Torrid.]

defs: A sensation of dryness in the throat associated with a craving for liquids, produced by deprivation of drink, or by some other cause (as fear, excitement, etc.) which arrests the secretion of the pharyngeal mucous membrane; hence, the condition producing this sensation.
quotes: Wherefore is this that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us, and our children . . . with thirst?
With thirst, with cold, with hunger so confounded.

defs: Fig.: A want and eager desire after anything; a craving or longing; -- usually with for, of, or after; as, the thirst for gold.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Thirsted; p. pr. & vb. n. Thirsting.]

defs: To feel thirst; to experience a painful or uneasy sensation of the throat or fauces, as for want of drink.
quotes: The people thirsted there for water.
My soul thirsteth for . . . the living God.
He seeks his keeper's flesh, and thirsts his blood.
